caption: An educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on the "Heat Exchanger." The top frames provide a textual definition, stating that "A heat exchanger is a device in which the cooling effect from cold fluid is transferred to precool the hot fluid." The text further explains that it can be a "two – fluid type or a three – fluid type" and that the process is "isobaric" because it occurs at a "constant pressure." As the presentation progresses to the bottom frames, visual diagrams are introduced. One diagram shows a heat exchanger with "Fluid A, in, hot" entering and "Fluid A, out, cold" exiting, accompanied by a temperature-length graph. The final frame adds a second fluid stream, labeling "Fluid B, out, hot" and "Fluid B, in, cold," with a corresponding blue curve added to the temperature graph. Each frame features the NPTEL logo and the text "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay."
